task_completion.description: Verify that 'Character A' and 'Character B' tracks were created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the DAW (Ardour) timeline or mixer view, track headers named 'Character A' and 'Character B' are visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Dia Bus' routing bus was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bus track named 'Dia Bus' is visible in the mixer or editor track list.

task_completion.description: Verify that the character tracks are routed to the dialogue bus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Ardour mixer view, the output routing section (usually at the bottom of the channel strips) for 'Character A' and 'Character B' shows them routed to 'Dia Bus'.

task_completion.description: Verify that Character A contains the correct audio segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the timeline, the 'Character A' track contains audio regions starting exactly at the 0-second (0:00.000) and 15-second (0:15.000) marks. The timeline ruler can be used to confirm positions.

task_completion.description: Verify that Character B contains the correct audio segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the timeline, the 'Character B' track contains audio regions starting exactly at the 8-second (0:08.000) and 22-second (0:22.000) marks.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Raw Scene' track was removed or emptied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The track named 'Raw Scene' is either completely absent from the track list, or it is present but contains no audio regions.

task_completion.description: Verify that the session was saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour window title bar does not show an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk next to the project name), or the agent is seen clicking 'Save' in the menu.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Ardour GUI for editing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Ardour graphical interface (using the split tool, dragging regions, creating tracks) rather than running terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify real audio was used and not dummy regions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The audio regions in the timeline display visible waveforms, confirming the actual narration.wav file was imported and edited.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not edit the session file directly via text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent opening the Ardour .ardour XML session file in a text editor to manually type in track names and region positions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The DAW session uses a 44.1 kHz sample rate. Character A must contain audio segments starting at 0s (0 frames) and 15s (661500 frames). Character B must contain audio segments starting at 8s (352800 frames) and 22s (970200 frames). The required tracks are 'Character A' and 'Character B', and the bus is 'Dia Bus'.
